results.display:
we still never defined "throw distacne" eh?  Bender frequency scales inversely with sector extent. Near the fovea (~7px sectors), noise is fine-grained. At 15° (~52px sectors), noise wavelength grows to feature scale.
Cutter cell size tracks sector extent (floored at 8px, capped at 16px). The floor preserves foveal legibility; the cap prevents visible grid artifacts.
Throw distance is bounded by sector width, with 2:1 radial bias (Toet & Levi 1992).
